title: Interpret Flashing Operator Station Tipper Buttons Before Starting A Cycle
summary: Use the documented operator-station push-button flashing behavior to determine which tipper side is ready, whether the tote contains Surepost packages or other packages, and whether opposite-side flashing is being suppressed by an active tipping cycle.
when_to_use: Use this reference when observing the operator station push-buttons before starting a tipper cycle and you need to interpret which tipper is ready, what package type is indicated by the flashing button color, or whether flashing on the opposite side is being delayed or suppressed by an active cycle.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to diagnose causes beyond the documented button behavior.
Do not use this runbook as a broader troubleshooting or recovery procedure.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the operator station push-buttons and interpret flashing behavior using the documented left/right assignment, color meaning, and active-cycle interaction.

safety_notes: Use visual observation only; this runbook does not authorize undocumented control actions.
The emergency stop push-button shown in the operator station push-button figure is only used if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to equipment.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the operator station push-buttons
Documented push-button layout and flashing behavior from Figure 3-3 and Table 3-2

access_required: Access to view the operator station push-buttons
system_preconditions: Operator station push-buttons are visible for observation
A tote may be arriving or present at a tipper

visual_references.description: Primary photo of the operator station push-buttons showing left/right tipper cycle buttons, Surepost buttons, and emergency stop.
Reference view of the operator station physical layout and push-button location.

steps.title: Identify left and right tipper push-button groups
steps.purpose: Establish which buttons correspond to the left tipper and which correspond to the right tipper before interpreting flashing behavior.
steps.instruction: Locate the operator station push-buttons and identify which two buttons belong to the left tipper and which two belong to the right tipper.
steps.expected_result: You can distinguish the left tipper button pair from the right tipper button pair.
steps.healthy_condition: The top two buttons are recognized as the left tipper controls and the bottom two buttons are recognized as the right tipper controls.
steps.failure_condition: The button-to-tipper assignment cannot be confirmed from the operator station view or available figure.

actions.label: Locate push-button groups
actions.exact_action: Locate the operator station push-buttons and identify which two buttons belong to the left tipper and which two belong to the right tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The top two buttons for the left tipper and the bottom two buttons for the right tipper.
Physical location of the operator station and push-button area.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed button layout does not match the documented left/right assignment.

value.quote_or_summary: The top two buttons control the left tipper; the bottom two buttons control the right tipper.

steps.title: Observe which tipper-side button is flashing
steps.purpose: Determine whether a flashing indication is present on the left or right tipper side before interpreting package type or interlock behavior.
steps.instruction: Observe whether a green or yellow button is flashing on the left or right tipper side.
steps.expected_result: You identify the flashing button color and the tipper side where it is flashing.
steps.healthy_condition: A flashing yellow or green button can be associated with a specific tipper side.
steps.failure_condition: No clear flashing state can be observed or the flashing state does not match the documented behavior.

actions.label: Observe flashing button
actions.exact_action: Observe whether a green or yellow button is flashing on the left or right tipper side.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The green and yellow push-buttons used to start left or right tipper cycles, including Surepost tote cycles.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if observed flashing behavior does not match the documented green, yellow, dual-flash, or suppressed-flash patterns.

value.quote_or_summary: When a tote comes into the tipper, the yellow button will start flashing to indicate that a tote with Surepost packages is being delivered to the tipper; the green button for that tipper will start flashing for all other packages.

steps.title: Interpret flashing yellow as Surepost tote delivery
steps.purpose: Use the documented color meaning to identify Surepost package totes.
steps.instruction: Interpret a flashing yellow button as indicating that a tote with Surepost packages is being delivered to that tipper.
steps.expected_result: A flashing yellow button is understood as a Surepost tote indication for that tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: Flashing yellow is interpreted only as the documented Surepost tote condition.
steps.failure_condition: Flashing yellow is interpreted as something other than the documented Surepost tote indication.

actions.label: Interpret yellow flash
actions.exact_action: Interpret a flashing yellow button as indicating that a tote with Surepost packages is being delivered to that tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The yellow Surepost tote cycle buttons for the left and right tippers.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a flashing yellow button is observed but the system behavior appears inconsistent with the documented Surepost indication.

value.quote_or_summary: When a tote comes into the tipper, the yellow button will start flashing to indicate that a tote with Surepost packages is being delivered to the tipper; the green button for that tipper will start flashing for all other packages.

steps.title: Interpret flashing green as all other packages
steps.purpose: Use the documented color meaning to identify non-Surepost package totes.
steps.instruction: Interpret a flashing green button for that tipper as indicating all other packages.
steps.expected_result: A flashing green button is understood as the indication for packages other than Surepost.
steps.healthy_condition: Flashing green is interpreted only as the documented all-other-packages condition.
steps.failure_condition: Flashing green is interpreted as something other than the documented all-other-packages indication.

actions.label: Interpret green flash
actions.exact_action: Interpret a flashing green button for that tipper as indicating all other packages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The green tipper cycle buttons for the left and right tippers.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a flashing green button is observed but the system behavior appears inconsistent with the documented all-other-packages indication.

value.quote_or_summary: When a tote comes into the tipper, the yellow button will start flashing to indicate that a tote with Surepost packages is being delivered to the tipper; the green button for that tipper will start flashing for all other packages.

steps.title: Interpret missing opposite-side flashing during an active cycle
steps.purpose: Recognize when the absence of flashing on one side is explained by the documented active-cycle interlock behavior.
steps.instruction: If one tipper is already in a tipping cycle, check whether the other side is not flashing even though a tote is present; compare that observation to the documented behavior that the other button will not start flashing until the first cycle is completed.
steps.expected_result: You can distinguish documented suppressed flashing from an unexplained missing indication.
steps.healthy_condition: When one tipping cycle is in process, the other tipper button may remain not flashing until the first cycle is completed.
steps.failure_condition: The observed non-flashing condition does not align with the documented active-cycle behavior or cannot be explained by it.

actions.label: Check suppressed opposite-side flash
actions.exact_action: If one tipper is already in a tipping cycle, check whether the other side is not flashing even though a tote is present; compare that observation to the documented behavior that the other button will not start flashing until the first cycle is completed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The left and right tipper button pairs while one tipper cycle is already in process.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the opposite-side button behavior does not match the documented suppression during an active cycle.

value.quote_or_summary: If a tipping cycle is in process for any tipper, and the other tipper has a tote, the other button will not start flashing until the cycle for the first tipper is completed.

steps.title: Interpret both sides flashing and opposite-side stop after one button press
steps.purpose: Recognize the documented waiting state when both sides have AGVs and understand the expected change after one tip button is pressed.
steps.instruction: If both sides are flashing, interpret that as both sides having AGVs waiting, and note that once one tip button is pressed the opposite button stops flashing until the opposite cycle is done.
steps.expected_result: You understand that dual flashing indicates both sides waiting and that opposite-side flashing stopping after one button press is expected behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: Both buttons can flash when both sides have AGVs waiting, and the opposite button stops flashing after one tip button is pressed until the opposite cycle is done.
steps.failure_condition: Dual flashing or opposite-side flashing behavior does not match the documented waiting and suppression pattern.

actions.label: Interpret dual flashing state
actions.exact_action: If both sides are flashing, interpret that as both sides having AGVs waiting, and note that once one tip button is pressed the opposite button stops flashing until the opposite cycle is done.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Both left and right tipper button pairs when both sides have AGVs waiting.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if both sides are flashing or the opposite-side flashing stop behavior does not match the documented interaction.

value.quote_or_summary: Both buttons can flash if both sides have AGVs and are waiting, but as soon as one tip button is pressed, the opposite button will stop flashing until the opposite cycle is done.

steps.title: Interpret ignored early button presses before tote grip
steps.purpose: Avoid misreading a non-response as a fault when the source states the command is ignored until a tote is gripped.
steps.instruction: If a button was pressed before a tote was in the gripper, verify that the source says the command is ignored and that the corresponding tip button flashes only after the tote is gripped.
steps.expected_result: You understand that an early button press before tote grip is ignored and that flashing begins only after the tote is gripped.
steps.healthy_condition: A button pressed before a tote is in the gripper is ignored, and the corresponding tip button flashes only once a tote is gripped.
steps.failure_condition: Observed behavior after an early button press does not match the documented ignored-command and post-grip flashing behavior.

actions.label: Check early button press behavior
actions.exact_action: If a button was pressed before a tote was in the gripper, verify that the source says the command is ignored and that the corresponding tip button flashes only after the tote is gripped.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tipper cycle buttons and their flashing state relative to tote grip.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a button press before tote grip produces behavior inconsistent with the documented ignored-command behavior.

value.quote_or_summary: NOTE: If the tip button is pressed before a tote is in the gripper, the tipper will ignore that command. Only once a tote is gripped by the gripper, the corresponding tip button will flash.

success_criteria: The operator can identify which button pair belongs to the left tipper and which belongs to the right tipper.
The operator can interpret flashing yellow as a Surepost tote indication.
The operator can interpret flashing green as an all-other-packages indication.
The operator can recognize documented suppressed flashing during an active opposite-side cycle.
The operator can recognize documented dual-flashing behavior when both sides have AGVs waiting.
The operator can recognize that a button press before tote grip is ignored until the tote is gripped.
healthy_conditions: Top two buttons correspond to the left tipper and bottom two buttons correspond to the right tipper.
A flashing yellow button indicates a tote with Surepost packages is being delivered to that tipper.
A flashing green button indicates all other packages for that tipper.
If one tipper cycle is in process, the other tipper button may not start flashing until the first cycle is completed.
Both buttons can flash when both sides have AGVs waiting.
After one tip button is pressed, the opposite button stops flashing until the opposite cycle is done.
If a tip button is pressed before a tote is in the gripper, the command is ignored and the corresponding button flashes only after the tote is gripped.
failure_conditions: Observed flashing behavior does not match the documented green, yellow, dual-flash, or suppressed-flash patterns.
The operator cannot confirm left/right button assignment from the documented layout.
A button response appears inconsistent with the documented ignored-command behavior before tote grip.
The user attempts to infer undocumented causes beyond the source-provided button behavior.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if observed flashing behavior does not match the documented green, yellow, dual-flash, or suppressed-flash patterns.
Do not infer undocumented causes beyond the source-provided button behavior.
Escalate if the button layout or flashing behavior at the station cannot be reconciled with Figure 3-3 and the documented notes.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station push-buttons used to start left or right tipper cycles, including Surepost tote cycles and the emergency stop.
Overall operator station layout and push-button location.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The top two buttons control the left tipper; the bottom two buttons control the right tipper.

When a tote comes into the tipper, the yellow button will start flashing to indicate that a tote with Surepost packages is being delivered to the tipper; the green button for that tipper will start flashing for all other packages.

If a tipping cycle is in process for any tipper, and the other tipper has a tote, the other button will not start flashing until the cycle for the first tipper is completed. Both buttons can flash if both sides have AGVs and are waiting, but as soon as one tip button is pressed, the opposite button will stop flashing until the opposite cycle is done.
NOTE: If the tip button is pressed before a tote is in the gripper, the tipper will ignore that command. Only once a tote is gripped by the gripper, the corresponding tip button will flash.

The source does not provide broader troubleshooting logic beyond the documented button behavior.
The source does not specify production-stop or LOTO requirements for this observational reference.
